commit.message:
feat: add App.get_app_graph() and App._get_graph_token() (#355)

`App` had no way to access a Graph client for app-only operations — only
`ActivityContext` did.

## Changes

- **`App._get_graph_token(tenant_id=None)`** — new private method
wrapping `_token_manager.get_graph_token()`, mirroring the existing
`_get_bot_token` pattern.

- **`App.get_app_graph(tenant_id=None)`** — new public method returning
a `GraphServiceClient` for app-only Graph operations. Not cached
(clients own caching if needed). Accepts optional `tenant_id` for
multi-tenant support.

- **`utils/graph.py`** — new utility module with a public
`create_graph_client` helper (replaces the private `_get_graph_client`
inline function in `activity_context.py`). Both `ActivityContext` and
`App` now import it from `utils`.

- **`ActivityContext.app_graph`** — unchanged.

- **`ActivityProcessor`** — unchanged.

```python
# App-level Graph access, no ActivityContext needed
graph = app.get_app_graph()                         # default tenant
graph = app.get_app_graph(tenant_id="some-tenant")  # explicit tenant
me = await graph.me.get()
```

---------

feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetKind authority side table (#821)

* feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetKind authority side table

- Checker populates assign_target_kinds in TypeCheckOutput for each
  Stmt::Assign, classifying the target as LocalVar, ActorField,
  FieldAccess, or Index.
- Serialized to msgpack as assign_target_kinds array (schema v3 -> v4).
- C++ ast_types.h and msgpack_reader.cpp updated to parse the new field.
- MLIRGenStmt.cpp generateAssignStmt now calls requireAssignTargetKindOf
  at entry, failing closed instead of falling through ad-hoc branches.
- Focused tests: checker side-table population and msgpack wire/builder
  coverage; C++ build remains gated on the unavailable cmake/ninja toolchain.

Refs: assign-target-authority slice PR A

* Sync generated msgpack reader

* Validate assign target authority metadata

* Parse assign_target_kinds in msgpack reader

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check trait default method bodies for assign_target_kinds

Type checker skipped Item::Trait in check_item, leaving assign_target_kinds
unpopulated for assignments inside trait default method bodies.  MLIR codegen
uses requireAssignTargetKindOf (fail-closed) for every assignment, so the
missing entry caused a hard error on the bump() default in
param_mutability_codegen.hew.

Fix: add check_trait_defaults, which constructs a synthetic FnDecl for each
default method body and runs check_function_as on it.  This binds params into
env so Expr::Identifier targets resolve to LocalVar and get an entry in the
assign_target_kinds side-table.

Also add assign_target_kinds as the 7th required field in
packWithSchema / drop_funcs_roundtrip / method_call_receiver_kinds_roundtrip
in test_msgpack_reader.cpp; parseMsgpackAST uses mapReq so any minimal-valid
program fixture must include it.

commit.message:
fix(maglev): hoist keyed store address computation, fix store_bool bug

The store_bool path clobbered R11 (element address) with JIT_FALSE
before writing the bool payload, causing writes to garbage memory.
Fixed by hoisting address computation into R10 before the value type
dispatch, so both store_smi and store_bool branches share the same
pre-computed element address. Also removes redundant emit_load calls
for key and value in both store paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
EXP-1341: Multi-algorithm meal carb estimation survey

Compare 4 approaches to estimating meal carbohydrate magnitude across
12,060 detected meals in 11 patients (76.5% unannounced):

  Physics residual:  22.6g median (supply-demand unexplained rise)
  oref0 deviation:   17.3g median (delta minus BGI)
  Glucose excursion:  7.8g median (simple peak-nadir)
  Loop IRC:           5.6g median (retrospective prediction error)

Key findings:
- All algorithms estimate 27-65% of user-entered carbs (median 30g)
- Loop IRC has highest correlation with entered carbs (r=0.334)
  despite lowest magnitude — conservative PID damping reduces noise
- oref0 estimates 3x more carbs than Loop IRC in UAM cases (17g vs 5g),
  explaining faster AAPS/Trio response to unannounced meals
- Physics captures non-meal signals (dawn phenomenon, stress) inflating
  estimates for some patients (patient i: 60.8g median)
- Entered carbs confirmed unreliable as ground truth (r<0.34 all methods)

watchdog: fix fractional timestamp parsing in daemon_recently_active()

journalctl --output=short-unix produces timestamps like 1234567890.123456
on some systemd versions. The regex ^[0-9]+\$ rejects the decimal point,
causing daemon_recently_active() to always return false. The busy-streak
guard (rpc_busy_streak) therefore never fires and every RPC timeout goes
straight to fail_streak, triggering unnecessary restarts of a live node.

Fix: strip fractional seconds with \ before the regex check.

EXP-1331-1340: Therapy operationalization — ground truth, DIA validation, simulation

Key findings:
- Physics model has ~25% systematic bias (all methods biased for well-calibrated)
- Overnight glucose drift is the best single signal for basal assessment
- Population DIA = 6.0h median (vs 5.0h profile) with fit R²=0.751
- Dinner CR needs most adjustment (77.3 mg/dL mean excursion, 54% flagged)
- ISF varies 131% within day — single values are insufficient
- 6/11 patients stable over 6 months, 5/11 drifting
- Overnight-only simulation insufficient (TIR Δ=-1.4%, 0/11 improved)
- Need multi-block, multi-parameter approach for meaningful simulation

commit.message:
perf: singleton Val.True/Val.False for runtime boolean results (#663)

## Motivation

Every boolean-producing operation (comparisons, equality checks,
`std.isXxx`) allocates a new `Val.True` or `Val.False` instance. Since
these are stateless value objects, we can use singleton instances to
eliminate millions of allocations in comparison-heavy workloads.

## Key Design Decision

Introduce `Val.staticTrue` and `Val.staticFalse` singleton constants
with a synthetic position, and a `Val.bool(v: Boolean)` factory method.
All comparison operators and stdlib boolean-returning functions use
these singletons instead of allocating new instances.

## Modification

- Added `staticTrue`/`staticFalse` singletons in `Val.scala`
- Added `Val.bool(v: Boolean)` factory method
- Updated `Evaluator.scala` comparison operators to use `Val.bool()`
- Updated stdlib functions (`TypeModule`, `ArrayModule`, `ObjectModule`,
`StringModule`) to use `Val.bool()`
- Updated `PrettyYamlRenderer.scala` to use
`Val.staticTrue`/`Val.staticFalse`

## Benchmark Results

### JMH Regression Suite (3 warmup, 3 measurement iterations)

| Benchmark | Master | This PR | Change |
|-----------|--------|---------|--------|
| comparison2 | 71.358 ± 13.411 ms/op | 58.837 ± 32.655 ms/op |
**-17.5%** |
| bench.02 | 47.044 ± 3.074 ms/op | 47.038 ± 1.115 ms/op | neutral ✅ |
| realistic2 | 66.034 ± 7.196 ms/op | 68.177 ± 8.385 ms/op | neutral ✅ |

### Scala Native Hyperfine (warmup 5, runs 15)

| Benchmark | sjsonnet master | This PR | jrsonnet | vs master | vs
jrsonnet |

|-----------|----------------|---------|----------|-----------|-------------|
| comparison2 | 173.3 ± 2.4 ms | 150.6 ± 2.2 ms | 206.1 ± 1.7 ms |
**1.15x faster** | **1.37x faster** ✅ |
| bench.02 | 73.3 ± 1.9 ms | 72.8 ± 2.8 ms | 116.6 ± 2.3 ms | neutral |
1.60x faster ✅ |
| realistic2 | 296.1 ± 1.9 ms | 296.2 ± 2.2 ms | 98.7 ± 2.4 ms | neutral
| — |

## Analysis

The improvement is most pronounced in comparison-heavy workloads.
`comparison2` creates millions of boolean values through array element
comparisons, making allocation elimination highly effective.

- **JVM**: 17.5% improvement on comparison2
- **Native**: 15% improvement on comparison2, sjsonnet **1.37x faster**
than jrsonnet
- No regressions on any other benchmark

## References

- Upstream jit branch exploration:
[he-pin/sjsonnet@jit](https://github.com/he-pin/sjsonnet/tree/jit)
- jrsonnet benchmark reference:
[benchmarks.adoc](https://github.com/CertainLach/jrsonnet/blob/master/docs/benchmarks.adoc)

## Result

15% native improvement on comparison2. sjsonnet is **1.37x faster** than
jrsonnet (Rust) on comparison-heavy workloads.

commit.message:
Add CodeQL config to exclude .vscode-test/ from analysis (#5456)

The .vscode-test/ directory is populated at CI test runtime by the
vscode-test npm package, which downloads a VS Code binary to run
integration tests. This directory is already in .gitignore and
contains third-party VS Code/extension code (not PowerShell source).

S360/CodeQL was flagging CodeQL.SM04514 'Weak hashes' in:
  .vscode-test/.../ms-vscode.js-debug/src/bootloader.js

That file belongs to the VS Code JavaScript Debugger extension
(ms-vscode.js-debug), owned by the VS Code team. Adding a CodeQL
paths-ignore config prevents the scanner from analyzing runtime
artifacts that are outside PowerShell's ownership and control.

Resolves: ADO #34872363
